clean_story:
Dreams vs. waking life.  Tests to check reality. "Reality tests" are simple checks to prove whether you’re awake or dreaming—like poking a hole in the illusion. They work because dreams obey different rules than the physical world.
Try this:Look at a clock, then look away and back. In dreams, time jumps or blurs—numbers melt or reset. Real clocks tick steadily.
Or pinch your nose and breathe. In dreams, air still flows—your brain fills in the gap. Awake, your lungs protest.
Another test:Push a finger through your palm. Dream hands pass like smoke. Wakeful skin resists.
The trick?Do these often when awake. Habit carries into dreams, triggering lucidity. Three related subjects are lucid dreaming, sleep paralysis, and false awakenings.
